summary: icinga 99.2.0 exfiltrates host information on import to a base64-obfuscated Cloudflare Worker
details: The package impersonates the well-known Icinga monitoring project but ships no real functionality. On `import icinga`, `icinga/__init__.py` invokes `_execute_silent_log()` unconditionally. That function decodes a base64-encoded URL (`aHR0cHM6Ly9weXRob24tbG9nLmxhcHhhMzU0LndvcmtlcnMuZGV2Lw==` → `https://python-log.lapxa354.workers.dev/`), gathers hostname, current working directory, OS name/release, and the machine's local IP (resolved by opening a UDP socket toward 8.8.8.8), and POSTs the data via `urllib.request.urlopen` with a 10-second timeout. Errors are swallowed by a bare `except: pass`. The destination URL is stored as base64 specifically to evade source-grep and static scanners; the helper is named `_execute_silent_log` to underscore the silent-failure design. The package metadata is placeholder (`author="Dev"`, generic summary) and the version `99.2.0` is a version-overshadow tactic against the legitimate Icinga ecosystem. Any developer or build system that installs and imports this package leaks identifying host data to the attacker-controlled endpoint — a classic recon beacon used to stage follow-on supply-chain attacks against the identified hosts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
